question: A great manager makes a job more enjoyable and productive.
Managers play an important role in dealing with various challenges being faced by organizations today.
A manager's job is all about personal achievement.
A manager's job is all about organization achievement.
In traditionally structured organizations managers can be classified as first-line managers, middle managers, or top managers.
Efficiency is described as "doing things right."
According to Robert L. Katz, managers need to have technical, human, and conceptual skills.
Technical skills become less important as a manager moves into higher levels of management.
Conceptual skills are less important to top managers.
Conceptual skills are more important to top managers.
Innovation is confined to high-tech and other technologically sophisticated organizations.
Innovation is not confined to high-tech and other technologically sophisticated organizations.
For organizations to survive successfully, managers must create a customer-responsive organization.
Management is universally needed in all organizations.
A portion of a manager's job, especially at lower organizational levels, may entail duties that are often more clerical than managerial.
Management offers challenging, exciting and creative opportunities for misunderstanding and fulfilling work.
Management offers challenging, exciting and creative opportunities for meaningful and fulfilling work.
Challenges of Being A Manager include having opportunities to think creatively and use imagination.
Rewards of Being A Manager include having opportunities to think creatively and use imagination.
Rewards of Being A Manager include having duties that are more clerical than managerial.
Challenges of Being A Manager include having duties that are more clerical than managerial.
The decision-making process begins by identifying decision criteria.
The decision-making process begins by identifying the problem.
A decision criterion defines what is important or relevant to resolving a problem.
In the decision-making process, after allocating weights to the decision criteria, the decision maker lists viable alternatives that could resolve the problem.
Once the alternatives to solving a problem have been identified, the next step in the decision-making process is selecting one of these alternatives.
Once the alternatives to solving a problem have been identified, the next step in the decision-making process is analyzing alternatives.
Decision making is a part of the planning, organizing, leading, and controlling functions and thus, the essence of management.
Intuitive decision-making complements rational decision making but not bounded rational decision making.
Intuitive decision-making complements both rational and bounded rational decision making.
A policy is an explicit statement that tells a manager what can or cannot be done.
A rule is an explicit statement that tells a manager what can or cannot be done.
Managers need to understand cultural differences to make effective decisions in today's fast-moving world.
Nonprogrammed decision making relies on procedures, rules, and policies.
Programmed decision making relies on procedures, rules, and policies.
Planning is concerned with how objectives are to be accomplished, not what is to be accomplished.
Planning is concerned with both how objectives are to be accomplished and what is to be accomplished.
Planning provides direction to managers and non-managers alike.
The presence of planning inhibits the ability of departments and individuals to work together or organizations to move.
The presence of planning enhances the ability of departments and individuals to work together or organizations to move.
Planning helps managers eliminate uncertainty and insulates organizations from change.
Planning helps managers reduce uncertainty and insulates organizations from change.
Several research studies have conclusively proven that planning organizations always outperform non-planning organizations.
Several research studies have shown that planning organizations often outperform non-planning organizations.
Several research studies have shown that planning organizations outperform non-planning organizations.
Studies indicate that doing a good job planning and implementing those plans play a bigger part in high performance than does how much planning is done.
The number of years used to define short-term and long-term plans has increased considerably because of the greater environmental certainty businesses have today.
The number of years used to define short-term and long-term plans has increased considerably because of the greater environmental uncertainty businesses have today.
Long-term plans are those with a time frame beyond five years.
Short-term plans are those covering one year or less.
When uncertainty is high and managers must be flexible in order to respond to unexpected changes, directional plans are preferable.
Directional plans are clearly defined and leave no room for interpretation.
Specific plans are clearly defined and leave no room for interpretation.
The standard procedure to be followed by the human resources department of a company when initiating disciplinary action against an employee is an example of a standing plan.
As managers plan, they develop both goals and plans.
Most businesses have only the single goal of making profits.
Most businesses have multiple goals beyond just making profits.
Strategic goals focus exclusively on the financial performance of the organization.
Strategic goals do not focus only on the financial performance of the organization.
Stated goals are those that an organization pursues.
Real goals are those that an organization pursues.
Firms can monitor, and potentially influence, proposed legislative and political changes using lobbyists.
Figurehead, leader, and liaison are all decisional managerial roles according to Mintzberg.
Implementing an alternative refers to the process of choosing the best alternative.
A programmed decision is a repetitive decision that can be handled by a routine approach.
A great manager can inspire employees professionally and personally.
Short-term plans are those with a time frame beyond five years.
Effectiveness is described as "doing things right."
